title: MEDIUM: DNS rebinding TOCTOU bypasses SSRF private-IP checks
description:
In src/server.rs is_private_host_resolved() (line 1374), DNS is resolved to check whether a hostname maps to private/internal IPs. However, reqwest independently re-resolves the hostname when it opens the TCP connection. This creates a classic DNS rebinding TOCTOU (time-of-check-time-of-use) gap:

1. Attacker controls DNS for evil.example.com
2. First resolution (check): returns 1.2.3.4 (public) -- passes SSRF check
3. DNS TTL expires or attacker rotates the record
4. Second resolution (reqwest connect): returns 169.254.169.254 or 127.0.0.1

This affects every outbound HTTP request path that uses is_private_host_resolved() before a reqwest call:
- Actor key fetching (actor_cache.rs line 48)
- Follower actor document fetch (server.rs line 885)
- Card/link preview fetching (card.rs line 142)
- Media fetching (media.rs line 56)
- Delivery worker inbox validation (delivery.rs line 175)

The code already documents this risk (ponytail comment at line 1370). Impact is somewhat mitigated by redirect-following being disabled, which prevents the simpler redirect-based SSRF. But DNS rebinding does not require redirects.

Fix: The proper fix requires a custom reqwest DNS resolver (via tower-service or hyper's resolve trait) that pins the resolved IP from the check phase and rejects private IPs at connect time. Alternatively, a connect-time callback that inspects the resolved SocketAddr before the TCP handshake is established. reqwest's resolve() builder method can be used for this.
close_reason: Added SsrfSafeResolver (custom reqwest DNS resolver) that blocks private IPs at connect time. Eliminates DNS rebinding TOCTOU — no gap between check and TCP handshake. Pre-check in is_private_host_resolved retained as early-reject optimization.
